sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Skribnik, E. (2003). Buryat. In Janhunen, J. (ed.), The Mongolic Languages. Routledge.
preamble: The palatalization of the voiceless alveolar sibilant /s/ into a voiceless palato-alveolar sibilant /ʃ/ (sh), typically triggered by following high front vowels or glides.

examples.note: In Buryat, the sequences *si, *ci, *shi all merged as /ʃi/ (shi), creating a three-way neutralization. E.g., Proto-Mongolic *cinar 'quality' → Buryat shanar (via *c→s then *si→ʃi), *sira 'yellow' → Buryat shara. This is the environment where *s did NOT shift to /h/ (which only occurred before non-i vowels) and instead palatalized.
